To determine the range of screen widths where the button's area is at least 10 square inches, we formulate the inequality $P(x) \ge 10$. Substituting the given polynomial, we get $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 \ge 10$, which simplifies to $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 \ge 0$. Evaluating this function on the interval $[3, 6]$: at $x=3$, $P(3) = 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 7 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 7 = 11$, which is greater than 10. Since the derivative $P'(x) = 6x^2 - 10x + 3$ is positive for all $x \ge 3$ (the roots are approximately 0.4 and 1.2), the function is strictly increasing on the interval $[3, 6]$. Therefore, the inequality holds for the entire range $3 \le x \le 6$.

******
To find the screen width where the area is exactly 15 square inches, we solve $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15$, which simplifies to $2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0$. We test for rational roots using the Rational Root Theorem; potential roots are factors of 22 divided by factors of 2. Testing $x=3.5$ (or $7/2$): $2(3.5)^3 - 5(3.5)^2 + 3(3.5) - 22 = 2(42.875) - 5(12.25) + 10.5 - 22 = 85.75 - 61.25 + 10.5 - 22 = 13$. This is not zero.
